description: To hold something or someone as precious, treating with tender care and deep appreciation. 'Meropi' names the protective, nurturing aspect of love—the way we shelter what we value, attending to its wellbeing with devoted attention.
sound_symbolism: The word cradles what it names. 'Me' begins with nasal tenderness, holding close. Then 'ro' rolls with warm protection, care surrounding. Finally 'pi' seals with soft precision, what is cherished held safe.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a transitive verb: 'mi ru meropi' (I cherish you). Can specify what: 'mi lo nila meropi' (I cherish this day). Related to lothea (love) and themoka (treasure)—meropi emphasizes the protective, nurturing quality of valuing.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Cherishing practiced skillfully avoids possessive attachment—we hold dear without grasping, value without clinging.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Traditional cultures cherish relationships, crafts, and knowledge—what is valuable is tended across generations.
